description: Write at the lowest reading level appropriate for the audience (COGA recommends ≤8th grade for general audiences)
design_guidance: Use short sentences (≤20 words). Prefer active voice. Avoid jargon. Define acronyms on first use.
implementation_notes: Test with Hemingway Editor. Target Flesch-Kincaid Grade ≤8.
testing_notes: Have someone unfamiliar with the domain read and confirm understanding.

description: Don't invent novel interaction patterns when established ones exist. Users with cognitive disabilities rely heavily on learned patterns.
design_guidance: Place navigation in expected locations. Use standard icons. Don't repurpose familiar UI elements.
implementation_notes: Run heuristic evaluation against Nielsen Norman patterns.
testing_notes: Test with users who have cognitive disabilities or low digital literacy.

pattern_name: Avoid time limits or make them adjustable
description: Users with cognitive disabilities often need more time to process and respond. Time limits cause anxiety and errors.
design_guidance: Provide at least 20x the default time limit. Prefer no time limits for content creation tasks. Show a timer with clear remaining time.
implementation_notes: Implement adjustable session timeouts. Add aria-live countdown when < 60 seconds remain.
testing_notes: Test that time extension requests do not lose user data.

description: Present only the information and choices necessary for the current task. Extra options and information compete for attention.
design_guidance: Limit navigation items to 5-7. Group related content. Use progressive disclosure. Avoid autoplay and moving elements.
implementation_notes: Use prefers-reduced-motion to disable animations. Avoid auto-advancing carousels.
testing_notes: Test on mobile (smaller viewport forces prioritization). Time how long it takes to complete core tasks.

pattern_name: Support memory by reducing reliance on recall
description: Do not force users to remember information across steps. Show previously entered data. Provide context reminders.
design_guidance: Show order summary on each checkout step. Pre-populate forms with previously entered data. Show breadcrumbs. Provide undo.
implementation_notes: Implement autofill (autocomplete attributes). Store form progress in localStorage. Implement aria-describedby with contextual hints.
testing_notes: Verify that session restores partial form data on timeout/refresh.

pattern_name: Provide clear error prevention and recovery
coga_objective: Help users avoid and correct mistakes
description: Error messages must be specific and tell the user exactly how to fix the issue. Prevent errors before they happen with inline validation.
design_guidance: Error messages: say what happened + what to do. Never: 'Invalid password'. Validate as-user-types. Provide confirmation before irreversible actions.
implementation_notes: Use aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to error. Use role='alert' for live errors.
testing_notes: Test that every error message makes sense to someone with no technical background.

pattern_name: Use plain language for instructions and labels
description: Form labels and instructions must be simple and unambiguous. Avoid legal/technical language unless absolutely necessary.
design_guidance: Label the field with what you want not the data type. Put instructions before the input not after. Say 'required' in the label not just an asterisk.
implementation_notes: Pair asterisk with aria-label='required'. Add aria-describedby for hints.
testing_notes: Run a 5-second test: show the form for 5s then ask what each field wants.

pattern_name: Provide symbols and images alongside text
description: Icons and symbols aid comprehension for users with dyslexia or low literacy. They are not a replacement for text.
design_guidance: Use familiar icons. Add tooltips or visible labels to icon-only controls. Support AAC symbol sets where applicable.
implementation_notes: Never use icon-only controls without accessible name. Add title attribute for tooltip hover. Use aria-label for screen reader name.
testing_notes: Test icon comprehension: show icon without label and ask users what it means.

pattern_name: Avoid double negatives and complex conditionals
description: Double negatives and conditional logic are difficult to parse.
design_guidance: Rewrite: 'Do not disable notifications' -> 'Keep notifications on'. Avoid if-unless-then. Break multi-part conditions into separate steps.
implementation_notes: Test instructions with readability tools.
testing_notes: Ask a non-technical person to explain back each instruction in their own words.

coga_objective: Support different physical and cognitive approaches
description: Allow users to complete tasks by typing text searching selecting from a list or using voice/gestures.
design_guidance: Provide a text input + autocomplete dropdown as alternatives to complex selects. Support voice input. Do not require drag-and-drop (provide button alternative per SC 2.5.7).
implementation_notes: Add autocomplete tokens. Test paste and voice input work. Test that custom date pickers accept typed dates.
testing_notes: Test with voice control (Dragon NaturallySpeaking / Voice Control on macOS).

description: Navigation items should appear in the same place and same order on every page. Unexpected changes cause disorientation.
design_guidance: Do not reorder nav items based on most used. Keep logo link to homepage. Consistent breadcrumbs.
implementation_notes: Test on multiple pages that nav order and location are identical.
testing_notes: Automated: compare DOM order of nav elements across 5 pages.

description: Users may lose focus or have to stop. They need to resume where they left off without losing data.
design_guidance: Save form state every 30s. Email a resume link for long forms. Provide explicit save progress button.
implementation_notes: Implement autosave with aria-live='polite' announcing 'Progress saved'.
testing_notes: Test by refreshing mid-form and confirming data persistence.

description: Animated content moving elements auto-playing media and pop-ups compete for attention and can cause cognitive overload.
design_guidance: Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Stop all animations on user request. Do not auto-play video with sound.
implementation_notes: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { * { animation: none !important; } }
testing_notes: Test with browser set to prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.

pattern_name: Provide adequate whitespace and visual separation
description: Dense text and poor visual hierarchy make it hard to track position and parse content.
design_guidance: Body text: max 75 chars/line. Paragraph spacing >= 2x font size. Left-align body text - avoid justified.
implementation_notes: Implement CSS text-spacing overrides and verify nothing clips (SC 1.4.12).
testing_notes: Text-spacing bookmarklet test. Check no text is clipped at 1.5x line-height.

description: Users scan pages for structure before reading. Clear hierarchy guides attention and reduces cognitive effort.
design_guidance: One H1 per page. Headings should describe the section content. Use H2-H3 consistently. Do not skip heading levels.
implementation_notes: Automated: axe checks heading order. Manual: read headings list in NVDA (Insert+F7).
testing_notes: NVDA elements list: every heading should make sense out of context.

description: Not everyone can navigate hierarchical menus. Search lets users go directly to content they remember by keyword.
design_guidance: Add site search. Provide filter/sort on long lists. Show 'X results for Y'. Allow clear-all filters in one click.
implementation_notes: Implement search with role='search' landmark. Results list: aria-live='polite' with count announcement.
testing_notes: Test keyboard navigation through filter options and results.

description: Calling the same thing by different names ('cart' vs 'basket' vs 'bag') is confusing. Create a product glossary and stick to it.
design_guidance: Define a vocabulary list. Do not synonymize to sound varied - repeat the same word. Provide a glossary page or tooltip definitions for technical terms.
implementation_notes: Consider <abbr title='definition'>term</abbr> for inline definitions.
testing_notes: Audit the product for the same concept referred to by multiple names.

description: Pre-selected sensible defaults reduce decision fatigue. Autocompletion reduces typing burden and errors.
design_guidance: Pre-populate fields where possible. Offer autocomplete suggestions. Use sensible default form values. Show examples.
implementation_notes: autocomplete attributes on all personal data fields. Placeholder text: supplement (not replace) labels.
testing_notes: Test that autocomplete is not blocked. Verify placeholder text has sufficient contrast (3:1).

pattern_name: Provide text alternatives for audio and video
description: Some users process written text better than audio. Transcripts benefit people with ADHD cognitive disabilities and situational limitations.
design_guidance: Provide captions for all video. Provide transcripts for podcasts/audio. Offer text summaries for long videos.
implementation_notes: Caption format: WebVTT preferred. Include speaker identification and meaningful non-speech sounds.
testing_notes: Verify captions sync with audio. Test with captions on and sound off.

description: Multi-step processes should not require completion in a single session.
design_guidance: Send resume link via email. Save progress server-side with a unique ID. Clearly indicate where the user left off. Provide 'Save and continue later' button on every step.
implementation_notes: Implement server-side session persistence. Generate shareable resume URLs.
testing_notes: Test by logging out mid-process and resuming from a different browser.
